title: verbascum phlomoides var. australe
intro: Verbascum phlomoides var. australe, also known as the Tasmanian mullein, is a variety of the Verbascum phlomoides plant, a flowering perennial that belongs to the figwort family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help soothe skin irritations, wounds, and respiratory issues such as coughs and colds. The plant is rich in antioxidants, vitamins, and minerals, providing health benefits like reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and boosting the immune system. The herbal preparations of Verbascum phlomoides var. australe include infusions, decoctions, and salves, which can be used topically or taken internally to harness its medicinal properties.
